title: Build AI brand and content production skill
description: Create a private personal-agent-skills skill for Joe Black's AI development brand and content engine. The skill should reference the live site, local site source/content paths, personal design-system skill, platform playbooks, AI-assisted workflow, publishing guardrails, and metrics experiments. Originated as personal-agent-skills-8rs and moved into agent-marketplace per current working-directory policy.

title: Ingest cross-agent skills system as managed project
description:
The ~/.agents/skills/ directory is the source of truth for shared AI agent skills.
agent-marketplace should own, document, and maintain this system.

## Current State

- ~/.agents/skills/ = 25 skills, managed via ~/.agents/.skill-lock.json
- ~/.claude/skills and ~/.gemini/skills are symlinks → ~/.agents/skills/ ✅
- ~/.cursor/skills/, ~/.codex/skills/, ~/.opencode/skills/ are NOT symlinked ❌
- Full audit at: ~/.claude/handoffs/cross-agent-skills-system.md

## Work Items

1. Register ~/.agents/skills/ as a first-class project in agent-marketplace
2. Ensure `claude skill install` targets ~/.agents/skills/ (confirm skill-lock.json path)
3. Add a 'health check' command or script that validates all agent symlinks
4. Complete Phase 1 migration (cursor + opencode symlinks) — zero data loss
5. Plan Phase 2 migration (codex skills migration before symlinking)
6. Add agent-marketplace docs on how to install skills and maintain the shared system

## Acceptance Criteria

- All 5 agent configs (Claude, Gemini, Cursor, Codex, OpenCode) read from ~/.agents/skills/
- Health check script passes cleanly
- agent-marketplace README explains the symlink architecture

Repos: agent-marketplace, codex-review-bot, jobsearch-tracker, ext-tool, resume-builder, rpi-cluster-bootstrap, quart-sqlalchemy, coinaddr, pricing, cookiecutter-uv, cookiecutter-ds, couchdiscover. (helpers is now consolidated)

title: Add deprecation notice and archive idea-nursery repo
description: idea-nursery GitHub repo still has 6 open issues pointing to the old home. Add a deprecation notice to its README pointing to agent-marketplace, comment on each issue, then decide whether to archive the repo.

title: Update helpers project card GitHub link to nursery/
description: The helpers/idea-nursery project card in site/index.html links to agent-marketplace/tree/main/projects — update to nursery/ after the rename.

title: Fix nursery/ paths in GitHub issues #1-6
description: Issues #1-6 on agent-marketplace were created with projects/ paths before the rename to nursery/. Update issue bodies to point to nursery/<name>/ paths instead.

title: Catalog all projects in agent-marketplace into a discoverable reference table
description:
## Why

Agents (and humans) entering this repo have no quick orientation to what projects exist and what they do. A single reference table — project name, one-line summary, key entry points, and an 'agent can learn more at' pointer — would let any LLM or contributor get oriented in seconds.

## What needs to happen

1. Enumerate all projects: top-level directories and nursery/* subprojects (currently: claude-switcher, obsidian-user-base, openclaw-setup, orb-autostop, pieces-babysitter, pieces-metrics, site, installer).
2. For each project, gather: name, brief purpose summary, primary language/stack, status (active/incubating/stable), and where an agent should look to learn more (README path, CLAUDE.md section, skill file, etc.).
3. Decide on the best delivery format:
   - A sub-doc linked from the global CLAUDE.md (e.g. docs/PROJECTS.md)
   - A superpowers skill that surfaces the table on demand
   - Or both (doc as source of truth, skill as loader)
4. Write the table and wire it up so agents automatically get it loaded.

## Acceptance criteria

- Every project in the repo has an entry
- Each entry has: name, one-line description, stack, status, agent-entry-point
- The table is reachable from CLAUDE.md (direct link or via skill)
- An agent starting a new session can answer 'what projects are in this repo?' without reading code
acceptance_criteria: Every project has an entry; table linked from CLAUDE.md; new-session agent orientation works

title: Build agentic-repos catalog skill for global CLAUDE.md injection
description:
The joe-stack-preferences skill already has references/agentic-repos.md with a table of all Joe's repos. The goal is to make Claude reach for the right tool proactively without being told.

## Problem

Claude currently only knows about agentic repos if joe-stack-preferences is invoked. That skill is triggered by stack/architecture questions — not by general task requests that happen to touch a repo that already exists.

## Work Items

1. Evaluate whether agentic-repos.md inside joe-stack-preferences is sufficient or if a standalone skill makes more sense for discoverability.
2. If standalone: create `~/.claude/skills/agentic-repos/SKILL.md` that activates when someone mentions building something that already exists in Joe's ecosystem (PR reviewer, MCP server, marketplace plugin, etc).
3. Add a `### Agentic Repos` activation entry to global CLAUDE.md under `## Skills activations` so Claude auto-invokes it when the task domain matches one of these repos.
4. Define the trigger criteria clearly: what phrases / task types should activate this skill?
5. Keep the catalog current as new repos are created — consider a convention for adding new entries.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Claude, starting a fresh session with no prior context, suggests using an existing repo rather than building from scratch when the task clearly matches one.
- The skill activation is low-noise: it does not fire on every coding task, only when there is a plausible repo match.

title: Add tests for claude-switcher (claude-use.zsh)
description:
claude-use.zsh now has meaningful functionality (launchctl setenv, JSONC inline comment stripping, profile switching, status output, clear command) but zero tests. Add a test suite to verify all major behaviors so future changes can be made confidently.

Test coverage needed:
- _cu_persist_launchctl: sets vars via launchctl setenv, clears via unsetenv
- _cu_persist_vscode_env: JSONC stripping preserves string literals containing //, trailing comma removal, correct JSON output
- cu status: shows launchctl state (vertex / oauth token / none)
- cu clear: clears shell env AND launchctl vars
- Profile loading: vertex profile sets VERTEX keys, clears OAuth; OAuth profile sets token, clears VERTEX keys
- Array subscript fix: _cu_env[] works without quotes

title: Decide openclaw-setup secrets architecture
description: openclaw-setup has a complex secrets pipeline via 1Password + launchd. Open question: does this belong in the public nursery or a private repo? Decision affects whether it can be promoted publicly.

title: Verify obsidian-user-base bootstrap end-to-end
description: The 'just bootstrap' command in nursery/obsidian-user-base hasn't been run end-to-end since adoption. Verify paths are correct for Obsidian Vault and _wiki/ locations after the projects/ → nursery/ rename and repo move.

title: Replace placeholder writing links with real article URLs
description: The Writing section in site/index.html has 3 articles all linking to the Medium homepage. Wire up real URLs once articles are published, or replace with actual published pieces.

title: Run article idea extractor across Claude Code sessions
description: The extractor prompt lives at docs/idea-article_extractor.md. Run it against ~/.claude/projects/ session files and write output to docs/article-ideas.jsonl and docs/article-ideas-summary.md.
